claims.claim: Mokaev is no longer a top-10 caliber fighter.
claims.quote: I don't think he's like top 10 material anymore, to be honest.

claims.claim: Horaguchi has a weak chin which causes him to lose fights he is otherwise winning.
claims.quote: the problem with Horaguchi is he's so stupidly chinny, and this is not the first time it's been a problem for him. So, he could be winning all of a fight, but if he gets touched once, he's going down.

claims.claim: Vinicius Alegre is very elusive.
claims.quote: To be fair, he is very elusive.

claims.claim: Van has more power and better combination follow-ups than Horaguchi.
claims.quote: Van has more power than Horaguchi, is much better at following up on combinations

claims.claim: Van is chinny, but less so than Horaguchi.
claims.quote: while he's probably a little bit chinny as well, um, it's it's not the same level as like Horaguchi chinny.

claims.claim: Tyra's head movement is poor.
claims.quote: I'd be nervous because Tyra's head sucks.

claims.claim: Kape's takedown defense is suspect.
claims.quote: Kape's takedown defense is still a little bit suspect, honestly.

claims.claim: Abdulaev lacks nuance in his striking and relies on landing massive hooks or overhands.
claims.quote: There's no real nuance to his game whatsoever.

claims.claim: Abdulaev is capable of maintaining a high pace for 15 minutes.
claims.quote: he is pretty good at keeping up a pace for like the full 15 minutes if he needs to.

claims.claim: Nascimento has proven five-round cardio.
claims.quote: He does have proven five-rounds cardio

claims.claim: Bekzat's takedown defense is a significant concern.
claims.quote: Bekzat's takedown defense concerns me a lot on this side.

claims.claim: Bekzat sacrifices late-fight cardio for early-fight speed.
claims.quote: He definitely does slow down as the fight goes along, but that's a sacrifice that he makes for being just ridiculously fast early in the fight.

claims.claim: Matsumoto's defense is reliant on his volume, leading him to get hit clean frequently.
claims.quote: the biggest problem is that Matsumoto's defense is his volume. He gets hit clean a lot, especially early in the fights.

claims.claim: Bekzat is a dangerous one-punch knockout artist who is very light on his feet.
claims.quote: Bekzat is of course a an extremely dangerous one punch knockout artist. He will move around. He's very light on his feet

claims.claim: Bekzat tends to leap in with strikes, including uppercuts, combinations, and overhands.
claims.quote: He tends to leap in with strikes. So, that could be from uppercuts to quick combinations, overhands, etc.

claims.claim: Bekzat slows down as the fight progresses due to his high-speed output early on.
claims.quote: He definitely does slow down as the fight goes along, but that's a sacrifice that he makes for being just ridiculously fast early in the fight.

claims.claim: Matsumoto is a pressure-oriented fighter who relies on volume accumulation rather than one-shot power.
claims.quote: He is pressure-oriented and he's very well-rounded... he generally speaking just wants to break you with volume accumulation. He's not really going to hurt you with one shot.

claims.claim: Matsumoto's defense is poor, specifically because he gets hit clean often early in fights.
claims.quote: The biggest problem is that Matsumoto's defense is his volume. He gets hit clean a lot, especially early in the fights.

claims.claim: Matsumoto lacks the ability to stall out a round to recover after being hurt compared to Umar.
claims.quote: Even if Matsumoto survives the initial shot, he doesn't really have the ability to stall out the rest of the round to try to get his wits back like uh Umar does.

claims.claim: Danchenko has questionable fight IQ, specifically regarding decision-making during potential finishes.
claims.quote: He does have very questionable fight IQ though. There have been multiple times where he could get a finish and then just either went to the ground when he shouldn't have or or he did the wrong part of the body.

claims.claim: Bergon has a tendency to reset awkwardly, leaving him vulnerable to being caught.
claims.quote: he does have a tendency where he resets a little bit weirdly and can be caught when he does.

claims.claim: Islam Makhachev fights with his chin in the air, making him susceptible to being caught.
claims.quote: Islam is going to back up with his chin in the air and he's a lot chinnier than someone like Gaethje.

claims.claim: Paddy Pimblett's boxing defense is atrociously bad.
claims.quote: Paddy's boxing defense is atrociously bad too

claims.claim: Javier Reyes keeps his hands low to facilitate his flowing striking style.
claims.quote: He keeps his hands low so he can flow into his strikes more easily.

claims.claim: Awfully lacks knockout power, allowing opponents to ignore his striking threats.
claims.quote: He doesn't have any power, so opponents can for the most part just ignore it.

claims.claim: Ruziboev is vulnerable in the pocket because he is so rangy.
claims.quote: He can be very vulnerable in the pocket if you can get there because of the fact that he is so rangy.

claims.claim: Pulyaev lacks power in his hands.
claims.quote: he doesn't really have much power in his hands.

claims.claim: Pulyaev has a very durable chin.
claims.quote: He does seem to have a pretty crazy chin though, what we saw in the Ottobah fight at least.

claims.claim: Ruziboev is vulnerable in the pocket due to his rangy frame.
claims.quote: He can be very vulnerable in the pocket if you can get there because of the fact that he is so rangy.

claims.claim: Pulyaev lacks power in his hands.
claims.quote: he doesn't really have much power in his hands.

claims.claim: Pulyaev's upright stance leads to him getting hit more frequently.
claims.quote: Because he stands so tall, his movement is a little bit weird, which definitely can lead to him getting hit a lot more than you would think.

claims.claim: Pulyaev has a durable chin.
claims.quote: He does seem to have a pretty crazy chin though, what we saw in the Ottobah fight at least.

claims.claim: Yakhyaev's takedowns rely on back takes and slams rather than clean double leg finishes.
claims.quote: His takedowns usually transition into back takes and slams rather than like clean double leg finishes and such.

claims.claim: Yakhyaev's defensive performance in his last fight was not optimal.
claims.quote: His defense, as we saw last fight, not optimal, to say the least.

claims.claim: Yakhyaev's cardio is untested despite having gone five rounds previously.
claims.quote: His cardio is I'm going to say untested.

claims.claim: Johnny Walker improved his double leg takedown technique in his last fight.
claims.quote: in his last fight, he surprisingly stepped up the wrestling very significantly and had like good double leg shots.

claims.claim: Aslan's takedown technique and timing are questionable.
claims.quote: His takedowns are very mediocre though. He's explosive, but his technique and timing are kind of questionable.

claims.claim: Nolan's footwork is not spectacular, leading to him getting caught.
claims.quote: His biggest weakness is that his footwork isn't spectacular, so sometimes they can get behind, they can get him caught.

claims.claim: Makachev is prone to body shots.
claims.quote: Historically, he's been very prone when his opponents go to the body

claims.claim: Makachev has poor takedown defense.
claims.quote: he does not have good takedown defense.

claims.claim: Makachev has a very durable chin.
claims.quote: He does, however, have a crazy chin, though.

claims.claim: Abus has poor cardio.
claims.quote: Abus is a very well-rounded fighter with impressively bad cardio.

claims.claim: Makachev is prone to body shots.
claims.quote: Historically, he's been very prone when his opponents go to the body

claims.claim: Makachev has poor takedown defense.
claims.quote: he does not have good takedown defense.

claims.claim: Makachev possesses a very durable chin.
claims.quote: He does, however, have a crazy chin, though.

claims.claim: Abus has poor cardio that causes him to gas out even when inactive.
claims.quote: He will gas out standing still and doing absolutely nothing, though.

claims.claim: Abu Azaitar's submission offense is overrated.
claims.quote: I do think Abu Azaitar's submission offense is a little overrated.

claims.claim: Ikram Aliskerov has abysmal striking defense and chin durability.
claims.quote: his defense and chin are both abysmal.

claims.claim: Ikram Aliskerov has poor footwork and is slow.
claims.quote: His footwork is also pretty terrible and beyond that he's slow.

claims.claim: Bruno Ferreira is poor at controlling opponents on the ground.
claims.quote: He is bad at controlling on the ground

claims.claim: Charles Johnson performs poorly when forced to move backwards.
claims.quote: he does very poorly when moving backwards.

claims.claim: Charles Johnson is vulnerable to lead hook counters due to his tendency to crash into the pocket.
claims.quote: He is generally very open to lead hook counters because he will crash into the pocket.

claims.claim: Nazim is very shoulder roll dependent but uses an educated high guard to block strikes.
claims.quote: Uses a high car high guard and he is very shoulder roll dependent but it is a educated high guard so he makes sure to move it's actually block stuff.

claims.claim: Nazim is highly vulnerable to leg kicks because he focuses too much on his high guard.
claims.quote: Because he's so focused on the high guard though, he will just eat any like leg kick you throw at him whatsoever.

claims.claim: Nazim's takedowns are primarily double leg attempts and are not very high quality.
claims.quote: His takedowns are mainly double leg attempts. They honestly are not the greatest overall though.

claims.claim: Nazim consistently falls behind on the scorecards and is forced to make comebacks.
claims.quote: He does always fall behind on the scar score cards and have to make a comeback.

claims.claim: Camilo has extreme cardio issues.
claims.quote: He does seemingly have extreme cardio issues.

claims.claim: Camilo is vulnerable to being countered on the feet because he loads up on his strikes.
claims.quote: He can be countered on the feet because of how much he loads up.

claims.claim: Nazim has a worse chin than expected.
claims.quote: I think that Nazim has a much worse chin than than was expected.

claims.claim: Arman's striking entries are predictable and leave him open to the check left hook.
claims.quote: his entries are actually fairly predictable, and his striking itself leaves a lot of openings, especially to the check left hook

claims.claim: Shara Magomedov has negative grappling ability.
claims.quote: Shara has negative grappling.

claims.claim: Shara Magomedov is vulnerable when opponents close the distance because he keeps his hands low.
claims.quote: He keeps his hands low, so you can close the distance on him. So, if you can close the distance on him, he's very vulnerable.

claims.claim: Michel Pereira's cardio is extremely poor.
claims.quote: Cardio is actual garbage. Um it is worse than Abuze somehow, and that is saying a lot.

claims.claim: Fiziev suffers from the traditional Muay Thai issue of being overly proactive and gassing out.
claims.quote: He definitely has the traditional Muay Thai problem of being mainly proactive and gassing a lot.

claims.claim: Torres is vulnerable to being caught when he overextends into the pocket.
claims.quote: He does overextend when he enters and is a little bit weak in the pocket if his first shot doesn't land.

claims.claim: Torres has underrated wrestling and is strong from the body lock.
claims.quote: He also has underrated wrestling. He's very strong and likes to work from the body lock.

claims.claim: The fighter has a tendency to gas out due to a proactive, traditional Muay Thai style.
claims.quote: He definitely has the traditional Muay Thai problem of being mainly proactive and gassing a lot.

claims.claim: The fighter becomes rigid and slows down significantly as the fight progresses.
claims.quote: he's so rigid, he slows down so significantly.

claims.claim: The fighter is a dangerous first-round finisher who remains relaxed and does not chase the finish.
claims.quote: Torres is a very dangerous first round finisher. But, the interesting thing is you wouldn't expect it from how he fights because he's very relaxed and doesn't like chase the finish.

claims.claim: The fighter overextends when entering and is weak in the pocket if his initial strike fails to land.
claims.quote: He does overextend when he enters and is a little bit weak in the pocket if his first shot doesn't land.

claims.claim: The fighter possesses one-punch knockout power and uses elbows to punish opponents in the pocket.
claims.quote: He does have one punch knockout power, and he will use like elbows to punish in the pocket.

claims.claim: The fighter has underrated wrestling ability and is strong in body lock positions.
claims.quote: He also has underrated wrestling. He's very strong and likes to work from the body lock.

claims.claim: The fighter has a smaller margin for error because he has been rocked previously, though he recovers well.
claims.quote: he has a much smaller margin for error because Torres can take a few shots, and he has gotten rocked a few times, but he does recover well

claims.claim: The fighter has a habit of pulling back from right hands, making him susceptible to being caught by longer opponents.
claims.quote: Fiziev has a problem with pulling back from right hands in a way that longer opponents still end up catching and hurting him.

claims.claim: The fighter's takedown defense is wild, making him a difficult matchup for grapplers like BSD and Arman.
claims.quote: I think those are actually the better matchups for him because his takedown defense is so wild.

claims.claim: The fighter is known for bullying and attempting to injure amateurs in the gym.
claims.quote: he is known for basically trying to kill amateurs 50 lb lighter than him in the gym for no reason.

claims.claim: Torres has wild takedown defense.
claims.quote: his takedown defense is so wild.

claims.claim: Charles Oliveira is the hardest matchup for Torres because he could knock him out if Torres overextends.
claims.quote: I think that Charles is the hardest matchup for him because Charles could knock him out if he overextends.

claims.claim: BSD and Arman Tsarukyan are easier matchups for Torres than Charles Oliveira.
claims.quote: BSD and Arman are easier matchups for him in my opinion

claims.claim: Torres would be very scary if he fixed his footwork.
claims.quote: if Torres fixed his footwork, he would be very scary.

claims.claim: Ilya Topuria's win over Justin Gaethje was influenced by Gaethje damaging his eye early.
claims.quote: I do think that Justin's win was basically a fluke in the sense that he damaged at least his eye early.

claims.claim: Arman Tsarukyan's striking leaves him vulnerable to lead hooks.
claims.quote: his striking leaves a lot of weaknesses, especially to lead hooks.

claims.claim: Arman Tsarukyan may not fight again due to issues regarding betting.
claims.quote: He may not be allowed to fight because of the betting issue.

claims.claim: Fluffy Hernandez will be underrated following his loss to Sean Strickland.
claims.quote: I think Fluffy's going to get underrated after just a loss to Strickland who's literally the champion.

claims.claim: Justin Gaethje would break Conor McGregor in a late-round scenario.
claims.quote: prime for prime, I'd say Justin breaks him late.

claims.claim: Conor McGregor would finish Justin Gaethje within the first round.
claims.quote: Conor will put him out within one.

claims.claim: Jiri Prochazka is defensively unsound.
claims.quote: Jiri just has been so defensively not sound

claims.claim: Shane Collins possesses solid boxing skills.
claims.quote: He has solid boxing.

claims.claim: Michael Asuelu displays very low fight IQ.
claims.quote: I don't know how he fights with so low fight IQ.

claims.claim: Raposo is a solid boxer.
claims.quote: He is a solid boxer.

claims.claim: Mullins is arguably the best female BJJ practitioner in the world.
claims.quote: arguably the best female BJJ practitioner in the world

claims.claim: Christian Rodriguez's move to Extreme Couture allowed him to fight to his potential.
claims.quote: Moving to Extreme Couture was exactly what he needed. Like he actually fought to his potential

claims.claim: Navaho Sterling has very low fight IQ.
claims.quote: He has very low fight IQ because he just initiates grappling when he doesn't need to.

claims.claim: Horoguchi is a chinny fighter.
claims.quote: Horoguchi's a great fighter, just way too chinny.

claims.claim: Jamal Hill has terrible footwork.
claims.quote: he just has such terrible footwork

claims.claim: Gamrot's wrestling cannot be neutralized by Islam Makhachev.
claims.quote: Islam is not going to be able to wrestle against Gamrot. Like at this point we've kind of established you cannot wrestle against Gamrot.

claims.claim: Gamrot possesses superior technical skills compared to Chimaev.
claims.quote: skill wise, Gamrot's better.

claims.claim: Chimaev is more explosive than Gamrot.
claims.quote: Myev is just more explosive, for sure.

claims.claim: Hill has poor footwork.
claims.quote: he just has such terrible footwork though

claims.claim: Islam Makhachev will find it challenging to close the distance against Ian Machado Garry.
claims.quote: I do think it will be a little bit challenging, yeah.

claims.claim: Marlon Vera is susceptible to being taken down.
claims.quote: Marlon might be a little bit tougher seeing how easily he was getting taken down by Tyra.

claims.claim: Islam Makhachev will be unable to successfully wrestle Mateusz Gamrot.
claims.quote: I mean Islam is not going to be able to wrestle against Gamrot.

claims.claim: Gamrot has better boxing skills than Islam Makhachev.
claims.quote: I honestly respect Gamrot's boxing more than I do Islam's.

claims.claim: Nate Landwehr has poor defensive skills.
claims.quote: he had such bad defense that we didn't even get to that point

claims.claim: Basharat has high-level cardio that requires a high pace to break.
claims.quote: I think that if you're not going to really put the pace on him and break his cardio, which maybe Ewing could do, but I am not fully convinced yet.
